title: St. Charles County Region Overview
content:
St. Charles County spans nearly 600 square miles west of the Missouri River with 400,000+ residents. Blends historic character with suburban growth. Quick access to I-70, I-64/40, Routes 364 and 94.

Location: Northwest of St. Louis, bordered by Lincoln County (north) and Warren County (west). Missouri and Mississippi Rivers form natural boundaries. 30-60 min commute to STL City. Easy access to Downtown STL, Columbia, Jefferson City, Kansas City.

Character: Classic suburbs with master-planned communities, custom estates, and rural properties. Mix of new construction, older farmhouses, historic brick, and acreage. One of Missouri's fastest-growing counties.

County Seat - St. Charles: Historic Main Street, Frontier Park, Festival of the Little Hills. First Missouri State Capitol, Lewis & Clark Boat House, Katy Trail State Park.

Major Communities: Cottleville, Dardenne Prairie, Foristell, Lake St. Louis, New Town, O'Fallon, St. Peters, Wentzville, Defiance.

Amenities: Ameristar Casino, Family Arena, Lindenwood University, St. Charles Community College, riverfront trails. Strong school districts. Highest median incomes and lowest property taxes in Missouri.
